title: SPD of the normal-equation operator and the psd (singular) kernel case

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap G3 (Lemma L1).** The solver must prove $\mathcal A=(Z\otimes K)^TSS^T(Z\otimes K)+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$ is symmetric and positive (semi)definite, which is the prerequisite for CG.

$\mathcal A\succeq0$ always (sum of a Gram term and $\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$ with $K\succeq0$). **Strict positive definiteness requires $K\succ0$ and $\lambda>0$.** RKHS kernel matrices may be only psd / rank-deficient; then $I_r\otimes K$ is singular and $\mathcal A$ can be singular, so plain CG is not guaranteed to converge.

**Required:** prove symmetry and PSD; state the explicit hypothesis ($K\succ0,\lambda>0$) for SPD; handle the singular-$K$ boundary case (range/consistency argument or a $K+\epsilon I$ regularization). The $\lambda\to0$ limit should also be noted as singular.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved Gap G3 (Lemma L1): SPD of the normal-equation operator + singular-$K$ boundary case.**

I wrote `solution.tex` with four results (numerically verified, see below). With $\mathcal A=(Z\otimes K)^TSS^T(Z\otimes K)+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)=M^TM+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$, $M=S^T(Z\otimes K)$:

- **Lemma (symmetry + PSD), for all $K\succeq0,\lambda\ge0$:** $\mathcal A=\mathcal A^T$ since $M^TM$ and $(I_r\otimes K)^T=I_r\otimes K$ are symmetric; and $x^T\mathcal A x=\|Mx\|^2+\lambda\,\|(I_r\otimes L)^Tx\|^2\ge0$ using $K=LL^T$ and $I_r\otimes K=(I_r\otimes L)(I_r\otimes L)^T$.

- **Lemma (SPD), explicit hypothesis $K\succ0$ AND $\lambda>0$:** then $x^T\mathcal A x\ge\lambda\mu_{\min}(K)\|x\|^2>0$ (eigenvalues of $I_r\otimes K$ are those of $K$ with multiplicity $r$). I also prove **necessity**: $\lambda=0\Rightarrow\mathcal A=M^TM$ has rank $\le q<nr$ (singular); $K$ singular with $Kv=0\Rightarrow x=e_j\otimes v\in\ker\mathcal A$ for every $\lambda$ (singular), via the mixed-product rule.

- **Lemma (singular-$K$ boundary, $\lambda>0$):** $\ker\mathcal A=\ker(I_r\otimes K)$ (PSD-sum kernel = intersection of kernels, plus $\ker(I_r\otimes K)\subseteq\ker M$ since $Z\otimes K=(Z\otimes I)(I_r\otimes K)$), hence $\mathrm{range}(\mathcal A)=\mathrm{range}(I_r\otimes K)$. The RHS $b=(I_r\otimes K)\,\mathrm{vec}(B)$ lies in $\mathrm{range}(I_r\otimes K)=\mathrm{range}(\mathcal A)$, so the system is **consistent**; CG from $w_0=0$ keeps iterates in the $\mathcal A$-invariant subspace $\mathrm{range}(\mathcal A)$ and converges to the minimum-norm solution.

- **Remarks:** (a) practical $K_\varepsilon=K+\varepsilon I$ shift makes $\mathcal A_\varepsilon\succ0$ with $w_\star$ recovered as $\varepsilon\to0^+$; (b) $\lambda\to0$ is genuinely singular AND inconsistent ($b\notin\mathrm{range}(M^TM)$ in general) — so $\lambda>0$ is essential.

**Numerical checks (all passed):** SPD min-eig $>0$ for $K\succ0,\lambda>0$; min-eig $\approx0$ (singular) for rank-deficient $K$; $\ker\mathcal A=\ker(I_r\otimes K)$ (equal null dims, $\mathcal A\cdot\mathrm{null}(I_r\otimes K)=0$); consistency residual $\approx10^{-13}$ and CG residual $\approx10^{-12}$ in the singular case; at $\lambda=0$ the lstsq residual $\approx5.1>0$ confirming inconsistency. Closing as resolved.
